metadata.title: English Language Examination Questions - Iraqi Preparatory Schools (6th Grade)

sub_sections.instruction: Read this text carefully then answer (5) of the questions that follow:
sub_sections.text: Adam worked as a banker in a busy city. His job involved managing accounts, working with numbers, and meeting clients, but despite earning a good salary, he felt unsatisfied. He enjoyed helping people, but he wanted to make a real difference in their lives. One day, while helping a young couple open a savings account, Adam listened to their dream of starting a small business. They were excited but nervous about the difficulties they might face. Inspired by their hopes, Adam realized he could use his skills to help others like them. In his free time, Adam began volunteering. He offered advice to small business owners on how to manage their finances, save money and plan for the future. He also set up workshops to teach people how to manage money and make good financial choices. His work soon gained attention and the bank decided to support his efforts by offering free financial advice to local business owners. As Adam continued helping people, he felt more satisfied than ever. He realized that banking wasn't just about numbers, it was about using his knowledge to help others achieve their goals.
questions: What was Adam’s job?
What kind of advice did Adam give to small business owners?
The bank supported Adam’s efforts by giving him a promotion. (True / False)
What did he realize about his job as he helped others?
What inspired him to help the young couple?
Give the passage a suitable title.

sub_sections.instruction: Answer or complete (Five) of the following sentences using information from your text book:
questions: Why shouldn't an interpreter waste time thinking?
Wind power is clean energy because ............ .
Drivers mustn't speed because ............ .

sub_sections.instruction: Re-write the following sentences, follow the instructions between brackets. (Choose 10)

questions.text: My father asked somebody to install the new pipes. (Rewrite with the correct form of "have")
If I were you, I (not ask) his advice about anything. (Correct the form of the verb)
You’re very organized, so I think you will be a wonderful project manager. (Expectation: use "should")
I know they'll be here by 6 p.m. / I knew ............ . (Future in the past)
He (eat) a lot of fast food, but now he (cook) healthier meals. (Use the correct form of "used to" and the present or past simple)
Will you come to my birthday party? (Reported question) / She asked me ............ .
Unfortunately, I missed the bus this morning. That's why I was late to work. (Regret use "I wish")
Somebody is repairing the car at the moment. (Rewrite in the passive form)
The wedding (go) well because they (organize) everything very carefully. (Put the verbs in the correct form - past simple or past perfect)
Your sister is busy cooking for a party. You ask: (how long / cook?). Write a question using the words in brackets. Use the present perfect simple or present perfect continuous.
They've knocked down the restaurant. We had a meal there last year. / They've knocked down the restaurant ............ . (Combine using the correct relative pronoun)
Define a journalist. (Use "write articles for newspapers and magazines")

sub_sections.instruction: Choose the correct answer between brackets: (5 only)
questions: We have been waiting (for / since) two hours.
Can you (turn off it / turn it off)? It's too loud.
The movie was (frightened / frightening) that I couldn't sleep afterwards.
There isn't (many / much) sugar left in the jug.
I (need to / needn't) go to the store, as I've already bought everything.
I think she (will like / will be liking) the work.

questions.note: أسئلة الإنكليزي - السادس الإعدادي 2025 الدور الثاني

sub_sections.instruction: Read this text carefully then answer (5) of the questions that follow:
sub_sections.text: One sunny morning, an old woman was driving her small silver car through the busy streets of the city. She followed the traffic rules carefully. Suddenly, a police officer stopped her. He looked surprised and said, "Excuse me, madam. You seem quite old to be driving alone. Are you sure you're able to drive safely at your age?" The woman smiled politely and replied, "Officer. I have been driving for over forty years. I've never had an accident. My eyesight is still very good." The officer seemed unsure and asked, "Can you prove that your eyesight is still good?" Without hesitation, the woman opened her handbag and took out a small sewing needle and a piece of thread. Calmly and quickly, she put the thread through the small hole of the needle. She looked at the officer and said, "If I can do this, then I can see the road very well!" The officer was surprised and laughed. "That's amazing!" he said. "Your eyesight is better than mine!" He smiled respectfully and said, "That's very admirable, madam. Have a safe drive." The woman thanked him, smiled and drove away confidently. She felt proud that she could prove herself and she hoped people would learn that age does not always mean weakness or inability.
questions: Why did the police officer stop the old woman?
The woman had been driving for only five years. (True / False)
How did the woman prove her eyesight was good?
Where was the woman driving?
What lesson can we learn from the story? (a. Don't judge people by age. / b. Police officers are always right.)
Give the passage a suitable title.

sub_sections.instruction: Answer or complete (Five) of the following sentences using information from your text book:

If we don't take breaks, the pressure of life can affect both our ............... and ............... health.

sub_sections.instruction: Re-write the following sentences, follow the instructions between brackets. (Choose 10)

questions.text: This is a new computer, so I think it is faster than the other one. (Expectation)
I (clean) my room and I (find) $30 under my bed. (Put one verb in the past continuous and one in the past simple)
Have you finished your homework? She asked me ............... . (Reported question)
The leaves fell because of the wind. The wind ............... . (Rewrite the sentence with the correct form of "Make")
If it (be) sunny tomorrow, we will go for a picnic. (Correct the form of the verb)
You decided not to go to the park with your friends. Now you regret it. (Regret: use "If only")
(next / being / are / week / The / replaced / windows). (Put the words in the correct order to make a passive sentence)
The Tigris Hotel has a fabulous pool. (The pool opened two months ago) (Use the correct relative pronoun to make one sentence)
"I will arrive on time" I knew I ............... . (Future in the past)
She (not like) coffee, but now she (drink) it every day. (Use the correct form of "used to" and the present or past simple)
You meet a pilot. You ask: (how long / be a pilot?) (Write a question using the words in brackets. Use the present perfect simple or present perfect continuous)
............... An average of eight hours a night is about right. (Use an imperative to give advice)

options.text: Write a short essay giving your opinion about this statement: "Studying while you're working is worth all the hard work." Your essay should have a short introduction and a conclusion. Remember to use linking words and phrases. You should write (100 - 120) words.
Write (100 - 120) words on the advantages of studying English in Britain.

questions.note: Examination in English for Preparatory Schools - Biological Branch

sub_sections.instruction: Read this text carefully then answer (5) of the questions that follow.
sub_sections.text: Smoking causes lung cancer. Lung cancer is the number one cancer among men. Ninety percent of the people who get lung cancer die. Smokers have six times as much cancer of the mouth, tongue and throat as nonsmokers. Smoking gives people breathing problems. It harms the stomach. Smokers pollute the air. They breathe it out on their children and on their wives or husbands. Children whose parents smoke have more breathing and lung problems than other children. Smokers have the right to kill themselves by smoking if they want to. They don't have the right to kill their wives and children by smoking. Young people start smoking because everyone else is doing it. They feel grown up and independent. It helps them relax. Then it becomes a habit. It is very difficult to stop smoking, but the chance of getting lung cancer decreases only one year after one stops smoking. After ten years, the chance is almost the same as for nonsmokers.
questions: Why do young people smoke?
How many people die when they get lung cancer?
What happens to children when their parents smoke?
What is the chance of getting lung cancer ten years after a person stops smoking?
What does smoking do to the body?
Give the passage a suitable title.

options.text: Write a letter to your bank to complain about a withdrawal shown on your statement that you didn't make. Write (100 to 120) words.

Write (100 to 120) words on the advantages of studying English in Britain. These notes may help you: You can hear people speaking English all day, in many different situations. Lots of opportunities to practise speaking, especially if staying with a British family. Watch TV and films in English and listen to radio. Read English newspapers, advertisements, notices. Observe British way of life. Possibly stay with British family.

sub_sections.instruction: Read the following text carefully then answer (5) of the following questions.
sub_sections.text: Water is the most important liquid on Earth. We can't live without it. Without water we will die of thirst. Three-quarters of our planet is covered by water. Most is in oceans, seas and rivers. Some is frozen and forms ice caps at the North and south poles. Water goes round and round in a process called the water cycle. Clouds in the sky contain water as a gas - water vapour. This gas becomes liquid and falls to Earth as rain or snow. The rain brings water to seas, rivers and lakes. Water also goes into the soil and it is taken up by plants. Like all animals, you need water to stay alive. Your body loses water when you breathe or sweat. You can go for many weeks without food, but if you go for three or four days without water, you could die. Plants, too, need water to live. It is therefore necessary that we make use of our water carefully. We must try not to pollute the water sources in the world. We should also use water wisely so that there will be enough of it left for our future generation.
questions: What is the water cycle?
Three quarters of our planet is covered by water. (True / False)
When does your body lose water?
What do clouds in the sky contain?
Why is water very important to us?
Give a suitable title.
